description: SDK entry (createProfile / create_profile) conformance scenarios. Each scenario drives the SDK entry point as a subprocess with a given AGENT_* env and a handler of a named 'kind', then asserts the exact stdout lines and exit code. Both reference SDKs run the same fixture (Node: sdk/node/src/sdk.test.js, Python: sdk/python/tests/test_sdk.py) so the user-facing SDK contract cannot drift between languages. Handler kinds are implemented per-language in sdk_harness.js / _sdk_harness.py. All handlers are async — the shared contract both SDKs agree on. (Python additionally requires async; Node accepts sync too, but that divergence is out of scope for this shared fixture.)

scenarios.name: async-send-error-then-return
scenarios.note: Pins that send_error is non-terminal in both SDKs — the handler may continue and return a body. The bridge decides what to do with an AGENT_ERROR line + body; the SDK does not force-exit on sendError (only on a thrown ProtocolError).
